# Product Manager, AI Empowered Product Environment
...
🇩🇪 Germany – Remote
...
Trimble Inc. is a technology company that provides advanced solutions across various sectors including construction, transportation, geospatial, and natural resources. They empower industries to improve efficiency and profitability by leveraging real-time data insights, innovative software, and precise hardware. Trimble's solutions are used by millions of professionals globally, helping them to collaborate effectively and manage complex workflows, ultimately transforming how work gets done in diverse environments.
...
### 📋 Description
...
• Translate complex business opportunities and customer feedback into clear product requirements and scalable solution concepts. • Lead cross-functional collaboration with Engineering, Design, and Sales teams to ensure seamless product delivery and adoption. • Prioritize product initiatives based on customer value, technical feasibility, and strategic business importance. • Validate product direction through continuous customer interviews, usage analytics, and deep market research. • Monitor product performance and customer satisfaction metrics to identify opportunities for continuous improvement and innovation.
...
### 🎯 Requirements
...
• Proven experience in Product Management, ideally within B2B SaaS, logistics, or digital marketplace environments. • A customer-centric mindset with a track record of driving measurable business impact and solving complex problems. • Experience with AI-driven product concepts, workflow automation, or platform ecosystems. • Strong analytical skills with the ability to use data and market insights to drive decisive product outcomes. • Demonstrated ability to manage ambiguity and make clear decisions in fast-paced, uncertain environments. • Experience working closely with Engineering and Design teams within an agile development framework. • Knowledge of freight procurement workflows, including RFQs, tendering, and carrier management (Bonus Points).
...

...
# Senior Software Engineer
...
## Trimble Inc.
...
Trimble Inc. is a technology company that provides advanced solutions across various sectors including construction, transportation, geospatial, and natural resources. They empower industries to improve efficiency and profitability by leveraging real-time data insights, innovative software, and precise hardware. Trimble's solutions are used by millions of professionals globally, helping them to collaborate effectively and manage complex workflows, ultimately transforming how work gets done in diverse environments.
...
### 📋 Description
...
• Lead architectural design for a critical problem domain — including AI/ML systems, data pipeline infrastructure, search/retrieval, and platform scalability — owning key deliverables that drive customer success. • Collaborate with cross-functional teams — backend, infrastructure, security, and product — to integrate advanced technologies into our core platforms. • Spearhead the development of scalable, secure, and performant cloud-native solutions that solve critical real-world challenges for our customers. • Drive continuous improvement in DevSecOps, observability, CI/CD, and compliance automation to ensure high-quality and sustainable project outcomes. • Mentor and develop senior engineers, fostering a culture of technical growth and continuous improvement across the organization. • Unblock the hardest cross-team technical challenges, aligning multiple teams around shared technical goals and consistent architectural decisions.
...
### 🎯 Requirements
...
• 7+ years of professional software development experience in Python, JavaScript, Node.js, C#, Java, or equivalent languages. • Proven track record of designing and leading large-scale system architecture, with strong expertise in cloud-native architecture, containerization (Docker), and orchestration (Kubernetes). • Strong problem-solving skills with a passion for delivering "visionary pragmatist" results, converting ambiguous business problems into clear technical strategies that actually work. • Deep knowledge of DevSecOps, infrastructure-as-code, and CI/CD best practices. • Experience with API design, microservices patterns, distributed systems, and data pipeline architecture. • Exceptional written and oral communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.

results.title: Senior Software Engineer - Aplica pe eJobs
highlights:
Trimble Solutions (UK) Ltd
...
Desired skills: •Willingness to take on a leadership role and be one of the founding members of a new agile software team located in Brasov, Romania •Experience of Agile methodology –particularly as a Scrum Master, would be an advantage •Knowledge of the following: C/C++, C#/.NET and .NET Core, .NET 10, Client/server application development •Experience with database frameworks such as SQLite or MongoDB •Knowledge and skills regarding use of AI Tools for Accelerated Software Development such as Copilot and Cursor •Understanding of software development fundamentals such as OOP, SOLID & Design Patterns •Experience with technologies such as Kubernetes, Kafka, REST, GraphQL, gRPC and Protobuffers •DevOps experience in cloud environments such as AWS and Azure would be a great advantage •Fluent English and local office language (Romanian) Educational Requirements: •Bachelor's degree in computer science or other relevant technical field required, or equivalent combination of relevant education and experience •Masters degree in computer science or in relevant engineering discipline with significant computer science or CAE content, or an equivalent combination of relevant education and experience Attitude and enthusiasm are everything! If you have some but not all qualifications and feel motivated to apply, we encourage you to do so!
...
Do you want to make a difference? Help us to create software that improves people's lives! As a developer in Trimble you will be part of a global team, but located in Brasov, Romania, together with highly skilled, engaging teams of software engineers and developers. The new team will be responsible for developing Trimble Infrastructure Design Suite. An international software solution for designers, engineers, contractors and authorities in the construction industry. Trimble IDS is a ground-breaking solution connecting processes, people and information, built using Trimble Connect as a Common Data Environment for collaboration. Being part of the Civil Design and Engineering team, you will transform the way the construction industry works today. Develop the next-level of technology by bridging the gap between design and construct, as well as as-built data in infrastructure projects. The team collaborates closely with talented engineering teams working on other Trimble products such as SketchUp, SiteVision and Tekla. How you can contribute: •Take a leading role in establishing a new delivery team for Civil Design and Engineering. •Work across borders in an exciting international, multi-disciplinary, innovative environment •Contribution to the greater Trimble Engineering team with expertise and knowledge •Help define and encourage good Software Engineering principles and discipline •Participate in ground-breaking web and cloud technology initiatives •Make an impact on the development of products for tomorrow’s global infrastructure solutions. •Be part of a team with close customer collaboration where value is created and appreciated first hand. If you want to know more about the role, contact Engineering Director John Arthur at john_arthur@trimble.com for technical questions or recruiter Dominika Zagrodzka at dominika_zagrodzka@trimble.com for personnel-related questions. We offer you: •Versatile and challenging tasks in a global company •Innovative and pioneering culture •Passion to help each other succeed •Turn your great software ideas into (mixed) reality, from design, implement and test •Work on long term product development and make a lasting contribution •An informal and flexible working environment •Opportunities to grow inside the company •Inspiring work atmosphere where your competence is highly appreciated •Competitive salary •Attractive insurances including health insurance and a competitive pension plan

With 7+ years of Applied AI/ML experience and currently working as a Lead GenAI/ML Engineer, I specialize in designing and deploying advanced LLM-driven agentic applications. My focus is on building scalable, robust, and responsible AI systems that address complex business challenges through frameworks like LangChain and cutting-edge RAG techniques. I have a strong track record of translating product vision into cloud-native, microservices-based architectures, ensuring scalability and reliability from design to production. My technical leadership spans the full lifecycle, from solution design to observability and monitoring with tools such as Datadog and Databricks. Deeply engaged with the fast-evolving AI landscape, I stay current with fine-tuning, RLHF, and prompt engineering innovations. I’m equally passionate about mentoring and enabling teams, fostering technical excellence, and driving delivery of high-impact AI solutions. Beyond my role, I contribute actively to the ML and GenAI community on GitHub and Medium, believing in open collaboration and knowledge sharing as the cornerstone of building the future of AI.

results.title: Trimble's CEO on the AI Hype Gap, and What's Actually Working - Bricks & Bytes
highlights:
Trimble CEO Rob Painter sat down with Bricks & Bytes to explain how a 48-year-old positioning company thinks about AI. His argument is blunt: raw model power is getting cheap, but construction context is not. The wins he can actually point to are specific, like 60% productivity on takeoff and millions in labor savings on submittals, and the traps are mostly human, not technical. Here is what that means if you run projects, and if you build the tools.
...
Most AI talk in construction swings
...
unhelpful poles.
...
it changes everything
...
Friday, or
...
changes nothing. Painter, who runs a company of roughly 12,000 people with about 120 acquisitions behind it, lands somewhere more useful than both. He is bullish enough to have bought an AI contract-risk company, and skeptical enough to call generic AI overrated to its face.
...
That tension is the whole interview. Trimble just agreed to acquire Document Crunch, an AI platform that reads contracts and flags risk, and the plan is to drop it on top of the data Trimble already sits on rather than ship another standalone tool. Painter frames the company’s “right to win” around the breadth and depth of what it already does, plus industry context, not around chasing the frontier models.
...
Painter gets specific about where AI earns its keep, which is rarer than it should be. On takeoff, the unglamorous job of counting what a project actually needs, customers report around 60% productivity gains. He flags that this is more machine learning than generative AI, the kind of honesty this audience trusts. On submittals, one customer told Trimble they had saved millions in labor hours by handing the document grind to AI.
...
The front end of design is moving too. Trimble shipped natural language prompting into SketchUp and announced a Claude integration that lets people generate geometry from plain language and reference images, a shift we tracked in our weekly ConTech funding digest. Internally, Painter sees the bigger unlock across the whole product development cycle, not just the R&D and coding everyone fixates on, plus marketing, seller-call training, legal review, and support.
...
So is an incumbent like Trimble worried about OpenAI, Google, or Palantir wandering into construction? Painter starts with humility, because he has to. These companies are fast, they are powerful, and Trimble uses their technology to get better. He likes that the model companies and the venture money are in the industry, because it forces Trimble to keep earning the business.
...
Trimble signed an agreement to buy Document Crunch, an AI platform that reads construction contracts and flags risk such as payment disputes and compliance gaps. Painter’s logic is layering: put that risk intelligence on top of the huge data set already running through Trimble’s systems, rather than selling it as a separate tool. It signals where platform consolidation in construction is heading.(Source)
...
Three concrete places. Around 60% productivity gains on takeoff (mostly machine learning, not generative AI), millions in labor-hour savings for one customer using AI on submittals, and natural language design coming into SketchUp through a Claude integration. Internally, the bigger win spans the whole product development cycle, marketing, sales training, and support.(Source)
...
are fast and
...
uses their tech and learns
...
itize overnight. He
...
He thinks the gap between AI hype and real returns is mostly organizational, not technical. Saying the word AI does not create value. Capturing it means rethinking how teams work and changing the workflow around the tool. His mental shift went from GSD in 2024 to GSD plus intentionality in 2025, treating AI as a force multiplier for existing work rather than a magic fix.
...
. He thinks AI will make
...
easy, so there is a real opportunity
...
and unlock of unstructured
...
read on where
...
CEO sees the white space.

# Senior Software Developer, AI/ML
...
If you love building real systems that real customers use-and you get genuinely excited about LLMs, RAG, MCP, and agentic architectures-this role is for you.
...
The Applied AI team in Autodesk’s Data and Process Management (DPM) organization ships Cloud-Native AI agents that make our Product Data Management (PDM) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) workflows smarter and easier. This is a hands-on architecture role where you’ll design core platform patterns and also write code that lands in production.
...
We’re looking for a modern builder: Ambitious, Curious, and Practical-someone who has already shipped Scalable, Cloud-Native AI Applications to production and wants to level up further. You might have expertise in GenAI Applications, or you might have shipped traditional Machine Learning applications (recommendations, forecasting, anomaly detection) at scale and are ready to go all-in on agentic architectures. Either way, you build for reliability, quality, and impact.
...
AI-assisted engineering (how we deliver): We use modern AI coding agents (e.g., Cursor Agent, Claude Code, Codex or similar) as accelerators for planning, implementation, debugging, and documentation-but you own correctness, security, and maintainability.

...

...

...
• Traditional ML cloud applications (training pipelines, deployment, monitoring, iteration), and/or
...

...

...

...

...
• Demonstrated experience using AI coding tools to ship production systems, and the engineering judgment to verify and correct AI output (code review rigor, debugging skill, ownership of correctness)
...
• Experience building AI applications in the CAD or manufacturing domain
...

results.title: Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher | Joblume
highlights:
Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher | Joblume
...
# Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher
...
Remote, USA
...
Posted Jun 30, 2026
...
Full-time
...
## About Terra AI We are building the state-of-the-art AI platform for the discovery and development of clean energy and mineral resources. We bring the most advanced techniques in generative AI, foundation modeling, and autonomous decision optimization to tackle the most important problems in the geosciences. These systems can help more reliably identify critical resource deposits, more rapidly measure and characterize them, and design more efficient and sustainable production plans. We are backed by Khosla Ventures and other leading venture investors. We are now looking to grow our team from ~15 to ~30 by the end of the year to continue to mature our technology and support deployment with our world-class mineral and clean energy partners. Role description In the same way image generators have shown the remarkable ability to produce a diverse set of realistic pictures conditioned on a text prompt (and other inputs), we are developing a generative model that produces 3D geological models conditioned on geophysical surveys, bore hole measurements, and other forms of physical observation. The outputs of the generative model capture what we know and don’t know about the state of the subsurface, allowing explorers to make maximally informed decisions about how and where to explore for critical resources. We are looking for a talented deep learning engineer or scientist to lead the development of this model that will revolutionize decision making in the earth subsurface for a wide range of clean energy applications. Role Responsibilities Design, train, test, and iterate on diffusion models for 3D geological models Design, train, test, and iterate on an approach to for conditioning generation on geophysical data and other observations Inform the generation of synthetic data to improve model performance Adapt diffusion modeling approach to specific real-world projects in collaboration with project teams. Qualifications Required Qualifications: Extensive PyTorch Experience Deep understanding of PyTorch, including writing custom modules, optimizing training, and debugging issues in large-scale models. Expertise in Developing Large Deep Learning Models from Scratch Proven ability to design, implement, and train complex deep learning architectures from the ground up. Data Curation Skills Hands-on experience in creating, cleaning, and maintaining high-quality datasets tailored for machine learning applications. Strong Software Engineering and Design Experience Proficient in software development best practices, including version control, testing, and code optimization. Familiarity with designing scalable and maintainable systems. Bonus points if you: Experience with Generative Models Familiarity with generative architectures, particularly diffusion models, and an emphasis on posterior sampling methods. Knowledge of Transformer Architectures Experience building and training transformers, especially in applications involving 3D data. Scaling Models Across Large GPU Clusters Expertise in parallelizing models across multiple GPUs and optimizing distributed training pipelines. Cloud Infrastructure Expertise Experience setting up, managing, and optimizing cloud environments for machine learning workloads, including provisioning resources and managing costs.
